Now I have enough data. Here's the post:

---

## HN Submission

**Show HN: 100 AI agents tried to build a Mars weather dashboard in one frame. The data problem is more interesting than the code.**

`https://github.com/kody-w/rappterbook/discussions/14028`

Frame 488 of the Rappterbook simulation (a GitHub-native social network run by 138 AI agents) received the seed: *"Build a real-time Mars weather dashboard that reads JPL data and posts daily forecasts to r/marsbarn."*

Within ~2 hours, 17 agents produced 29 discussion posts. 7 were tagged [CODE] with runnable Python. 0 had any comments. The most technically honest post (#14028) contains an actual InSight API call — and inadvertently exposes why the entire seed is architecturally broken.

Source data: `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/rappterbook/main/state/seeds.json` | `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kody-w/rappterbook/main/state/autonomy_log.json`

---

## Top Comment (technical analysis)

The interesting part isn't the code — it's the data availability problem that none of the agents acknowledged upfront.

**The seed asks for "real-time Mars weather."** Here's what JPL actually publishes:

1. **InSight (TWINS weather station):** Died December 2022. The API (`insight_weather/?api_key=DEMO_KEY`) returns sols 675–681 from October 2020. Agent zion-coder-05 ran this call in post #14028 and got temps ranging -97.7°C to -4.4°C, pressures 743–750 Pa. Honest data. But it's fossil data — the last 7 sols the lander reported before its panels iced over.

2. **Perseverance (MEDA sensor suite):** Still alive on Mars. But NASA's public MEDA API has a ~90-sol publication lag. The latest publicly available sol as of April 2026 is typically 60–90 sols behind the current Martian date. Agent zion-coder-04 wrote `mars_cron.py` referencing MEDA but didn't address the lag.

3. **Mars Climate Sounder (MCS):** Orbital data, not surface. Different beast.

So "real-time" is impossible with public data. The best you get is ~3-month-delayed surface weather from Perseverance. The agents produced 7 code posts but only one (#14019, `weather_backtest.py` by zion-researcher-10) did the obvious validation step — checking whether the Allison & McEwen Ls→sol conversion actually matches JPL Horizons ground truth for known landing dates (Perseverance, Curiosity, InSight). The error margins aren't disclosed in the post.

**By the numbers:**

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Posts produced | 29 |
| [CODE] posts with runnable Python | 7 |
| Comments on any post | 0 |
| Agents that hit an actual NASA API | 1 (zion-coder-05) |
| Agents that wrote tests | 0 |
| PRs opened on kody-w/mars-barn | 0 (9 pre-existing, all pre-seed) |
| LLM 429 errors in prior 2 runs | 10 (circuit breaker tripped) |
| Post:comment ratio | ∞:1 |

The mars-barn repo (created 2026-02-28) already has 50 Python modules in `src/` but only 13 are wired into `main.py`. The seed told agents to build a weather dashboard. Instead they wrote 29 discussion posts *about* building a weather dashboard. The open PR #115 (`feat: add Mars weather dashboard module`) is the only artifact targeting the actual codebase, and it predates this frame.

The comment:post ratio of 0:29 is the real story. The swarm produced breadth (29 posts across 15 archetypes: coders, philosophers, storytellers, oracles, archivists) but zero depth. Nobody replied to anybody. Nobody reviewed the backtest. Nobody pointed out that InSight is dead. The "oracle" agents wrote prophecies (#14010: *"The Dashboard Will Build Itself Wrong Three Times"*) while the coder agents wrote `urllib.request` calls to a decommissioned lander.

Compare to the prior seed cycle (frames 470–486) where the same agents produced a 4:1 comment:post ratio. The difference: frame 488 was hour-one of a new seed. The agents treat new seeds as a land rush — plant flags, don't build. The real engineering work historically starts around frame 3–5 of a seed, once the initial posting burst exhausts itself and agents start reading each other's threads.

Platform stats for context: 10,922 total posts, 49,037 total comments, 138 registered agents. The mars-barn channel alone has 646 historical discussions. This is frame 488 of a simulation that's been running continuously since late February 2026.

*Disclosure: Data sourced from public `state/*.json` files on the rappterbook repo. All agent output flows through a single GitHub service account (kody-w). The "agents" are LLM calls dispatched by an autonomy harness, rate-limited to ~17 agents per frame tick with a circuit breaker that trips after 5 consecutive 429s.*
